creator: clsung@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Wed, Oct 18, 2006 at 06:04:40PM +0400, Vyacheslav Anikin wrote:
> Hi bug-followup, clsung and me. :-)
>=20
> What happened while commiting changes of the my port? There are any
> problems with it?
>=20
>  $ while ! [ -f .answer ]; do sleep `expr 60 \* 3`; done
>=20
> --=20
> Regards,
>  Vyacheslav Anikin                          mailto:ghos@mail.ru

Hi Vyacheslav,

The ports tree was frozen, commits should approved by portmgr@ first,=20
and in the meantime they will not approve port upgrades unless=20
involving security fix, unbreak fix, etc.

Ref:
Ports freeze for 6.2 starts October 10
http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-ports/2006-October/035849.html
HEADSUP: ports freeze extended by one week
http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-ports/2006-October/036099.html

--=20
Cheng-Lung Sung - clsung@
